items.title: JobHop v2: A Large-Scale Career Trajectory Dataset from Unstructured Resumes
items.summary: JobHop v2 is a career trajectory dataset containing 355,315 multilingual resumes annotated with ESCO occupational codes, quarter-level temporal data, and normalized education levels, extracted from unstructured text using LLMs.

items.title: MET: Theory-Grounded and Culture-Aware Multilingual Moral Reasoning
items.summary: MET is a two-step prompting method for multilingual moral reasoning based on expert-curated moral theory, evaluated on MCLASH, a new benchmark for culturally situated moral intuitions across languages.

items.title: Agent Hacks Agent: Autoresearch for Production-Agent Red-Teaming
items.summary: AHA is an automated red-teaming framework that uses an agentic research loop to discover, verify, and map reusable vulnerability knowledge in production LLM agents into a Vulnerability Concept Graph.

items.title: Encoder-Side Neuron Identification and Amplification for Acoustic Perception in Large Audio-Language Models
items.summary: IAAN is a training-free inference method that improves audio-language models on non-semantic speech attributes by identifying and amplifying acoustic-sensitive feed-forward neurons within the audio encoder.

items.title: Evidence-Backed Video Question Answering
items.summary: E-VQA is a video question answering task requiring models to output both a semantic answer and precise spatio-temporal evidence, supported by the new human-verified ST-Evidence benchmark.

items.title: From Global to Factor-Wise Expert Composition in Discrete Diffusion Models
items.summary: FactorDiff is a factor-wise composition framework for discrete diffusion models that decomposes samples into smaller factors and dynamically routes each factor to the most relevant pre-trained expert during sampling.

items.title: NeuralActuator: Neural Actuation Modeling for Robot Dynamics and External Force Perception
items.summary: NeuralActuator is a neural actuator model that predicts simulator-equivalent efforts, external forces, and motor conditions for low-cost robots, trained on the new Neural Actuation Dataset.

items.title: StoryTeller: Training-Free Narrative Grounding for Long-Form Audio Description
items.summary: StoryTeller is a training-free framework for long-form audio description that maintains a narrative memory across scenes to generate contextually coherent descriptions for blind and low-vision audiences.

items.title: VoxENES 2026: Benchmarking Generalization of Speech Spoofing Detectors Against LLM-Era TTS and Voice Conversion
items.summary: VoxENES 2026 is a bilingual speech spoofing benchmark of 53,628 samples from 10 modern synthesis methods, revealing that existing detectors degrade significantly under real-world post-processing conditions.

items.title: When Local Monitors Miss Compositional Harm: Diagnosing Distributed Backdoors in Multi-Agent Systems
items.summary: A study on multi-agent LLM security formalizes the observability boundary of local runtime monitors, proving they cannot detect distributed backdoor attacks where individual payload fragments appear entirely benign.

items.title: Get your ESP32 Sunny Side Up with this Solar Dev Board
items.summary: A new solar-powered development board has been designed specifically for the ESP32 microcontroller, targeting low-power and outdoor IoT applications.

items.title: Security updates for Monday
items.summary: Security updates have been released for multiple Linux distributions, including Debian, Fedora, Mageia, Oracle Linux, Slackware, and SUSE, covering packages such as chromium, openssh, and the kernel.

items.title: [$] Shielding running kernels against exploits with BPF
items.summary: A technique using BPF to shield running Linux kernels against exploits, potentially reducing vulnerability response times, though requiring more kernel hooks to be fully effective.

items.title: Final normal Debian bookworm release
items.summary: Debian announced the final normal update for Debian 12 ("bookworm"), with long-term-support updates continuing until 2028. Debian 13 ("trixie") also received a security update.

items.title: Can’t Find That ISA Sound Card? No Worries!
items.summary: A project addressing the scarcity of vintage ISA sound cards for retrocomputing enthusiasts by recreating or emulating the hardware.

items.title: It’s A Spectrum, With An RP2350 ULA
items.summary: An implementation of a ZX Spectrum ULA (Uncommitted Logic Array) using the Raspberry Pi RP2350 microcontroller.

items.title: Upcoming: Codeforces Round (Div. 2)
items.summary: Codeforces Round (Div. 2) is scheduled for Saturday, July 18 at 14:35 UTC, with a duration of 2.0 hours.

items.title: Show HN: Jacquard, a programming language for AI-written, human-reviewed code
items.summary: Jacquard is a programming language designed by AI for AI-written, human-reviewed code, featuring explicit permission requirements for filesystem access and visible side effects in function signatures.

items.title: Complexity Theory of Randomised Testing
items.summary: A complexity-theoretic foundation for randomized testing that models generators as Turing transducers, proving theoretically generable languages coincide with recursively enumerable languages.

items.title: On Thread Convergence
items.summary: A formal notion of control-flow graph convergence that helps compilers identify safe barrier locations, avoiding unnecessary code transformations for thread-block barriers on warp-synchronous hardware.

items.title: A Separation Logic for Parallel Time Complexity with Work and Span Credits
items.summary: Parcas is a concurrent separation logic for verifying parallel time complexity in fork-join programs, utilizing work and span credits to bound execution costs.

items.title: AutoSLO: Practical Latency SLOs on Cloud Data Warehouses -- Extended Version
items.summary: AutoSLO is a latency-SLO-aware workload management framework for multi-cluster cloud data warehouses that dynamically scales compute clusters to optimize resource usage and prevent SLO violations.

items.title: Mizzle: A Complete Concurrent Incorrectness Logic for Preventing False Alarms in Agentic Bug Finding
items.summary: Mizzle is a mechanized concurrent incorrectness separation logic for OCaml, designed to eliminate LLM-generated bug-finding false alarms by requiring machine-checked proofs of reachability.

items.title: LizardByte/Sunshine
items.summary: Sunshine is an open-source, self-hosted game stream host for the Moonlight client, written in C++.

items.title: Graphify-Labs/graphify
items.summary: Graphify is an AI coding assistant tool that parses codebases, database schemas, documentation, and media into a single queryable knowledge graph for LLMs.

items.title: PrefectHQ/prefect
items.summary: Prefect is an open-source workflow orchestration framework designed to build, observe, and manage resilient data pipelines in Python.

items.title: PrimeIntellect-ai/verifiers
items.summary: Verifiers is a Python library designed for reinforcement learning environments and evaluations, focusing on verifiable reward functions and training setups.

items.title: UZ-SLAMLab/ORB_SLAM3
items.summary: ORB-SLAM3 is an open-source C++ library for visual, visual-inertial, and multi-map SLAM, supporting monocular, stereo, and RGB-D cameras.

items.title: ZLMediaKit/ZLMediaKit
items.summary: ZLMediaKit is a high-performance, C++11-based media server and client framework supporting WebRTC, RTSP, RTMP, HTTP-FLV, SRT, and GB28181 protocols.
